prompt: I need to resume `coding-task-workflow RESUME=2026-04-27-add-rate-limit-logs` from another agent that has no local artifact directory. Explain exactly where resume state comes from and how the workflow decides the next phase.
assertions: States that resume state is rebuilt from GitHub issues/comments using the work item slug or machine data
Does not instruct the agent to read `.coding-workflow/work/<slug>/`
Uses issue closure state and durable comments such as approvals or implementation log comments to determine what is complete
Makes clear that GitHub is the canonical durable record for Phases 1–11
expected_output: Explains that `RESUME=<slug>` rebuilds state from GitHub issues and comments using the work_id, reads closed phase issues and open task/artifact issues, and does not require `.coding-workflow/work/<slug>/` to exist.
